Talks and conference sessions

Making complex technology more explicit, practical, and human.

I speak about agentic software engineering, multi-agent systems, and attention under pressure. This page contains a small curated selection rather than every talk idea or submission.

View my Sessionize profile Connect on LinkedIn
Showcased talks

Developed, presented, and ready for a new audience.

01
delivered Advanced engineers exploring multi-agent systems and orchestration.

Orchestrating a Game Dev Team Using Microsoft Agent Framework

A practical case study of specialized AI agents working as a game development team with shared artifacts, review gates, and human oversight.

Design clear agent roles, shared artifacts, review gates, and stop criteria.

View talk details
02
delivered Advanced software engineers and technical leaders adopting AI-assisted development.

The Agentic Developer

A practical model for making agent contributions reliable, observable, and safe through explicit intent, context, and execution.

Turn vague prompting into durable specifications, repository rules, and validation gates.

View talk details
03
delivered Developers and engineering teams looking for practical ways to reduce context switching and work with more attention.

Working Like a Buddha: Less Stress, More Joy

A practical session translating attention, simplicity, and presence into ordinary software engineering work without vague promises.

Recognize the cost of context switching in coding, reviews, meetings, and incidents.

View talk details
Invite me to speak

Conference, meetup, or an engineering team that wants to go deeper.

The sessions can be adapted to the audience, time slot, and technical depth. My Sessionize profile contains the current submission information; LinkedIn is the easiest place to start a conversation.

The digital zen garden

Build with clarity. Lead with care.

Software engineer, coach, speaker, and Zen teacher exploring how technology, leadership, and attention can help people build with more clarity, trust, and care.
